text.title: Vuln Triage SLA Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Vuln Triage SLA Template` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Severity MUST come from a published rubric (e.g. CVSS-3.1 vector + business-impact tier), not a reviewer hunch. Findings without a rubric trace are demoted to `triaged: false`.
rule.rationale: Hunch-severity collapses to the loudest reviewer; a rubric is auditable + defensible to regulators.

rule.statement: Each severity tier MUST list a concrete SLA in days (e.g. critical = 7d, high = 30d, medium = 90d, low = 180d). `as-soon-as-possible` and `next-quarter` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vague SLAs cause findings to age past compliance; concrete days are testable in CI.

rule.statement: Every accepted-risk waiver MUST carry an expiry date ≤ 6 months out and a named approver. Waivers without expiry are auto-revoked at first review.
rule.rationale: Permanent waivers are the #1 cause of stale risk; capped expiry forces re-evaluation.

rule.statement: Every status change (open → triaged → in-flight → fixed → waived) MUST emit an append-only log entry with timestamp + actor + rationale. Lost or rewritten log entries cause the artefact to fail review.
rule.rationale: Mutable triage history blocks compliance evidence; append-only trails are deterministic.

rule.statement: Severity matrix + SLA + waiver list MUST be reviewed on a published cadence (≤ quarterly). Stale matrix without review note is flagged at next ingest.
rule.rationale: Threat landscape moves; a frozen matrix locks the team to last year's threat model.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
